scenarios.title: Python Workflow Orchestration
scenarios.when_to_read: Tasks that mention Prefect flows, tasks, deployments, workers, orchestration, schedules, server, Cloud, blocks, events, automations, or the Prefect CLI.
scenarios.role: Routes Prefect usage and maintenance requests to focused SDK, deployment, CLI/server, API/settings, events/blocks/assets, and repo-development guidance.
scenarios.read_when: prefect, @flow, @task, prefect.yaml, work pool, worker, deployment, automation, block, asset, PrefectClient, PREFECT_API_URL, prefect-client, Prefect server, Prefect Cloud
scenarios.best_for: Authoring and debugging Prefect workflows, creating deployments, operating local or Cloud profiles, using the Python API client, validating automations/YAML, and modifying the Prefect repository safely.
scenarios.avoid_when: The task is about a deep provider integration package, UI-v2 React implementation, legacy UI, benchmark/load test infrastructure, or a different workflow orchestrator.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Choose `prefect` when Prefect is the target library or repository. Use the sub-skill routes for precise task families; create separate skills for UI or integration packages if deep coverage is needed.

scenarios.title: Python Repository Maintenance
scenarios.when_to_read: Tasks that require editing Prefect source, tests, docs, schemas, client packaging, or generated artifacts in the Prefect checkout.
scenarios.role: Provides Prefect-specific repository structure, AGENTS-scoped development rules, test selection, generated artifact handling, and package-boundary guidance.
scenarios.read_when: src/prefect, tests, docs/v3, client/pyproject.toml, uv.lock, AGENTS.md, just generate-docs, generated schema, prefect-client build
scenarios.best_for: Selecting focused tests and validation commands, respecting source/test mirrors and scoped AGENTS rules, updating generated docs/schemas/settings, and avoiding server state-transition anti-patterns.
scenarios.avoid_when: The request only asks how to use Prefect as an end user, or it targets frontend implementation details under ui-v2 or provider integrations under src/integrations.
scenarios.selection_guidance: Route maintainer edits to repo-development first, then cross-link to user-facing sub-skills only when behavior or docs need verification against public workflows.
